description: Periods where the FRED 10-year breakeven inflation rate (T10YIE) exceeded 2.5%, indicating elevated market-based inflation expectations. T10YIE is the difference between 10-year nominal Treasury yield and 10-year TIPS yield — the same spread our TIP/TLT ratio approximates. Using T10YIE rather than BLS CPI because our detector uses market-observable forward-looking signals, not backward-looking CPI prints. The 2008 oil shock (BLS CPI > 4% May-Oct 2008) is excluded: TLT rallied as flight-to-quality, suppressing our bond-weakness signal — a commodity-only CPI spike without bond-market confirmation is not the macro inflationary regime our detector targets.
dateSource: FRED series T10YIE (10-Year Breakeven Inflation Rate). https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/T10YIE. Daily, from Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

eventWindows.label: Post-COVID inflation: T10YIE first crossed 2.5% on May 10 2021 (2.49% → 2.54%). Fell back below 2.5% on Jun 28 2022 (2.48%), so last day at/above 2.5% was Jun 27 2022. Data verified from FRED T10YIE CSV.

passCriterion: Detector fires within 25 trading days of window start. Coverage >= 30% of window required. The market-signal window (T10YIE > 2.5%) is narrower than BLS CPI > 4% because signals lead and deactivate faster than the price index. 30% coverage reflects that the detector tracks the acute breakeven-expansion phase, not the full CPI-elevated period.
